mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent, invalidating the negative predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal spending and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative returns with a directional accuracy above 50% over a 2-year out-of-sample period, showing no interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects resilient manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, with a mean forward return below zero across all historical late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases show no predictive power for negative 5-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2.0 percent, robust aggregate demand sustains earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0 percent, breaking the positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment signals a deteriorating labor market that compresses consumer spending and equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual labor deterioration signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered cases, invalidating the negative predictive relationship and interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, a pause or reversal in federal funds rate hikes signals peak policy restriction, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate pauses during tightening cycles fail to produce positive 60-day returns, with mean forward returns remaining negative across all tightening episodes since 2000.
